paper_title: ESR and Radiocarbon Dating of Snail Shells in Fluvial Terraces

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing snail shells and sediment samples for ESR and luminescence dating from various fluvial terraces.

steps.description: Samples of ESR dating (snail shells and the surrounding sediment within 30 cm in diameter) and for luminescence dating were collected during geological field mapping.
steps.searchable_summary: collect snail shells and sediment samples from fluvial terraces

steps.description: Shells were cleaned manually, measured for thickness using a micrometer or REM, ground by hand, and sieved to a final grain size of 125 to 250 µm.
steps.searchable_summary: clean, measure, grind, and sieve snail shells

methods.method_name: ESR Measurements and Equivalent Dose Calculations
methods.description: Procedure for determining the equivalent dose (D E) using ESR measurements on snail shells.

steps.description: A multiple aliquot additive-dose procedure was applied for ESR D E determination using 18 to 20 aliquots per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: apply multiple aliquot additive-dose procedure for ESR D E determination

steps.description: The D E was derived from the analysis of the dating signal at g = 2.0007.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze ESR dating signal to derive D E

methods.description: Analysis of radiocarbon and luminescence data to verify ESR dating results.

steps.description: Two Late Würmian snail shells were dated using the radiocarbon (14 C) method and calibrated using the atmospheric 'IntCal13' calibration set.
steps.searchable_summary: perform radiocarbon dating and calibration on snail shells

steps.description: Luminescence samples were prepared and measured for D E estimation using the standard SAR protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and measure luminescence samples for D E estimation
